ques: Search for the latest preprints about 'quantum computing'.
Search for the latest research papers on quantum computing submitted to ArXiv within the last two days.
Look up the most recent papers related to 'cs.CL', select one and show its abstract.
Locate the most recent research paper about 'Algebraic Topology' under Mathematics published on ArXiv. Provide the title of the paper, the name of the authors, and the abstract.
Find the most recent research papers in Astrophysics of Galaxies. How many papers have been announced in the last day?
Search papers about "quantum computing" which has been submitted to the Quantum Physics category on ArXiv. How many results in total. What if search in all archives?
How many figures and tables are in the paper "On the Sentence Embeddings from Pre-trained Language Models"?
Find the most recent paper submitted on machine learning in the Computer Science category posted on ArXiv.
What is the latest news on ArXiv?
Find the latest research paper about neural networks published on ArXiv which has been submitted within the last week.
Visit ArXiv Help on how to withdraw an article if the submission is not yet announced.
For Non-English submissions, do I need to provide a multi-language abstract, if need, answer the separator between the multiple abstracts.
Find store in arXiv Help, tell me how many styles of arXiv Logo Shirt are available?
How many articles on ArXiv with 'SimCSE' in the title?
On ArXiv, how many articles have 'SimCSE' in the article and are originally announced in October 2023?
Searching Chinese Benchmark on ArXiv, how many papers announced in December 2023 mention being accepted for AAAI 2024?
Locate the latest research about gravitational waves that were uploaded to ArXiv this week and provide a brief summary of one article's main findings.
Find the paper 'GPT-4 Technical Report', when was v3 submitted?
Download the paper 'Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain Question Answering'. How many formulas are in the article and which one is the loss function?
Which university maintains and manages ArXiv. Accessing the university's website from ArXiv, how many underegraduate students are currently at the university.
Find the latest paper on 'machine learning in the Statistics section of ArXiv and provide its abstract.
Search for papers on 'neural networks for image processing' in the Computer Science category on ArXiv and report how many were submitted in the last week.
Locate the ArXiv Help section and find instructions on how to subscribe to daily listing emails for new submissions in a specific category.
Determine how many articles with the keyword 'autonomous vehicles' were published in the 'Electrical Engineering and Systems Science' section of ArXiv yesterday.
Identify the most recent paper related to 'graph neural networks' on ArXiv and determine the affiliation of the first author.
Browse the ArXiv store and let me know how many different types of merchandise are available.
Search for papers related to 'climate change modeling' on ArXiv and find out how many have been published in the Earth and Planetary Astrophysics (astro-ph.EP) category in the last week.
On ArXiv, what categories does Economics include, and what are their abbreviations?
Search 'Poly encoder' by title on ArXiv and check whether the articles in the search results provide HTML access.
On ArXiv, search for papers with 'Neural Network Optimization' in the title published in 2023, and provide the number of such papers.
Look up the submission guidelines on ArXiv for submitting a paper and tell me the formats for figures.
Search ArXiv for papers with 'Graph Neural Networks' in the abstract that were submitted between Jan 1, 2024, and Jan 3, 2024, and determine how many of these papers have more than five authors.
Locate the latest paper on ArXiv within the 'Nonlinear Sciences - Chaotic Dynamics' category, summarize the abstract and note the submission date.

Query ArXiv for the latest research article in the category of Systems and Control under Computer Science. Summarize the main objective or hypothesis presented in the paper and provide the names of the authors.
Search for the most recent paper related to non-commutative geometry submitted by an author with the first name John. Provide the title and the abstract.
Retrieve the latest research paper in Quantum Physics from ArXiv and provide the title, author(s), and date of submission.
Search 'CVPR 2023' and 'CVPR2023' through journal ref on ArXiv to see how many results there are respectively.
Find the names of people in ArXiv's Leadership Team.
Find the ArXiv Blog on the ArXiv website and summarize the content of its latest article.

Search the title 'GPT-4 Technical Report' and access this paper through HTML format. Read the paper on this page and tell me what is 'one of the main goals of developing such models' mentioned in the Introduction.

How many articles are there on each of the three most recent announce days in the Solar and Stellar Astrophysics section of ArXiv. Choose one at random and answer its title and when the first version was uploaded?
Find the button to share arxiv non-profit store and follow the QR code to share the shop. Then add arXiv Forever short sleeve (XL) to your cart.
Find an article published between 1 January 2000 and 1 January 2005 that requires Support Vector Machines in the title and its Journey ref is ACL Workshop.
Search papers by author 'Yann LeCun' on ArXiv. How many papers appear in the result list?
Use the Author field to search for 'Geoffrey Hinton' on ArXiv. Open the first paper in the result list and report its arXiv ID and the number of authors.
Search by author for 'Bengio' on ArXiv and report the titles of the three most recently submitted papers in the results.
On ArXiv, search by author for 'Danqi Chen' and look up the paper 'SimCSE: Simple Contrastive Learning of Sentence Embeddings'. Report its arXiv ID.
From the ArXiv author search for 'Jason Weston', open the paper whose title begins with 'Poly-encoders' and report its primary subject code.
Open the ArXiv author page for 'Tianyu Gao' (URL /author/Tianyu%20Gao) and report how many papers are listed for that author.
Search ArXiv by author for the surname 'Wang'. Report the total number of result papers shown for the query.
Use advanced search on ArXiv to find papers where the author field contains 'OpenAI' and report the title of the first result.
Search ArXiv by author for 'Andrej Karpathy' and report the total number of result papers.
On ArXiv, search by author for 'Goodfellow' and identify the affiliation listed on the first result's abstract page.
Browse the 'Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (cs.CV)' category on ArXiv. How many papers are listed in the recent listing?
Go to the 'Cryptography and Security (cs.CR)' category on ArXiv. Open the first paper in the new listing and report its title and primary subject.
On ArXiv, navigate to the 'High Energy Physics - Theory (hep-th)' archive page and report the description text shown for the category.
Open the ArXiv 'Computer Science (cs)' archive page and list all of its subcategory codes.
On ArXiv, open the category 'Information Retrieval (cs.IR)' and report the latest announce date shown on the page.
Navigate to the 'Optimization and Control (math.OC)' category on ArXiv and report the title of the top paper in the most recent listing.
Visit the 'Quantum Physics (quant-ph)' archive page on ArXiv and report how many papers are currently in that category overall (paper count badge).
On ArXiv, open the category 'Statistics - Machine Learning (stat.ML)' and list the first five paper titles shown in the recent listing.
From the ArXiv homepage, click into the 'Robotics (cs.RO)' archive and report the date displayed in the 'Today on arXiv' banner.
On ArXiv, open the 'Astrophysics of Galaxies (astro-ph.GA)' archive and report the number of papers in the listing's first page.
Use ArXiv advanced search to find papers submitted between 2024-01-01 and 2024-12-31 in the cs.CL category whose title contains 'language model'. How many results are returned?
On ArXiv advanced search, filter papers in cs.CV that were submitted in the past month (relative to the mirror today date) and report the total count.
Use ArXiv advanced search to find papers in 'math.NT' submitted between 2013-01-01 and 2013-12-31. List the titles of the first three results.
On ArXiv, restrict search results to the past 7 days and category cs.LG (relative to mirror today). Report the count of results.
Use the ArXiv advanced search form with start date 2025-10-01 and end date 2025-12-31, category cs.AI, and 'reinforcement' in the all-fields search box. Report the number of matching papers.
Search ArXiv for papers in cs.RO submitted in the last 30 days (mirror today is the reference). Report the title of the top match.
On ArXiv advanced search, filter by date range 2019-01-01 to 2020-12-31 with 'transformer' in the abstract, and report the number of results.
Restrict ArXiv search to category quant-ph submitted in the past year (relative to mirror today). Report how many papers match.
Use ArXiv advanced search with start date 2026-02-01 and end date 2026-02-28 and category cs.CL, then open the first result and report its title.
On ArXiv, run an advanced search for papers in eess.IV submitted between 2026-01-01 and 2026-03-31 and report the count of results.
Open the paper with arXiv ID '2104.08821' on ArXiv. Report the first sentence of its abstract.
Open arXiv:2303.08774 (GPT-4 Technical Report). Quote the first sentence of the abstract and report the number of authors listed.
Open the paper page for arXiv:1905.01969 on ArXiv. Report the journal_ref value and the comments field on the abstract page.
Open the abstract page of arXiv:2011.05864. Identify how many figures and tables are reported in the paper's comments field.
On the abstract page of arXiv:2004.04906, report the list of subject categories (subjects field) for the paper.
Open arXiv:2104.08821 and report the DOI field if it exists.
Open arXiv:0404.00123 and report the submission date and the value of the journal_ref field.
Visit the abstract page of arXiv:2303.08774. Report the primary subject area code and the announce_date.
From the ArXiv homepage, run a search for the keyword 'contrastive learning', open the top result, and quote the first 30 words of its abstract.
On ArXiv, open arXiv:2011.05864 and report the full list of authors in display order.
On ArXiv, search 'reinforcement learning' and sort the results by 'Most downloads'. Report the title of the top result and its download count.
Run an ArXiv search for 'transformer' and sort by 'Most starred'. Report the top result's arXiv ID and star count.
On ArXiv, search 'graph neural network' and sort the results by 'Most viewed'. Identify the top result's title.
Open the ArXiv search for category cs.CL with the sort option 'Most downloads'. Report the top three titles.
On ArXiv, list papers in cs.CV sorted by Most viewed and report the view count of the top paper.
On ArXiv, search 'diffusion model' and sort by date ascending. Report the title of the oldest result.
On ArXiv, search for 'embeddings' and sort by 'Most starred'. Report the top result's arXiv ID, title, and star count.
On ArXiv, run an empty search filtered by category cs.LG and sort by 'Most downloads'. Report the top paper's title and download count.
On ArXiv, search for 'attention' and sort by 'Most viewed'. Report the title and view count of the top result.
Log in to ArXiv with email alice.j@test.com and password test1234. Navigate to the Library page and report the names of the folders shown.
Log in as alice.j@test.com / test1234 on ArXiv. Visit the Library, open the 'Research' folder, and report how many papers it contains.
Log in to ArXiv as bob.c@test.com (password test1234). Add arXiv:2104.08821 to your Library under the folder 'Reading List' from the paper's abstract page.
Log in to ArXiv as carol.d@test.com / test1234. From the Library page, create a new folder named 'Survey 2026' and report the new total folder count.
Log in to ArXiv as david.k@test.com / test1234. Remove the first paper currently shown in your Library list and report the new library count.
On ArXiv, log in as alice.j@test.com / test1234. Move one paper from the 'Research' folder to a new folder called 'Highlights' and confirm the move on the Library page.
Log in to ArXiv as alice.j@test.com / test1234 and add a note 'foundation model' to one of the existing items in your Library; verify that the note appears on the Library list.
On ArXiv, register a new account (use any email + username, password 'test1234'). Then add arXiv:2104.08821 to your Library under a folder called 'NLP'.
Log in as bob.c@test.com / test1234 on ArXiv. From the Library page, rename one folder and report both the old and new folder names.
On ArXiv, log in as carol.d@test.com / test1234. Open the abstract page of arXiv:2303.08774 and click 'Save to Library' (folder 'Reading List'). Confirm the paper now appears in your Library.
Log in to ArXiv as david.k@test.com / test1234, open the abstract page of arXiv:2104.08821, and use the Star button. Then visit the Starred page and confirm the paper is listed there.
Log in as alice.j@test.com / test1234 on ArXiv. Visit your Starred Papers page and report how many papers are currently starred.
Log in to ArXiv as bob.c@test.com / test1234. Unstar one of your existing starred papers and report the new starred count.
On ArXiv, log in as carol.d@test.com / test1234. Toggle the star on arXiv:2011.05864 (if not starred, star it). Report whether the paper is now starred.
Log in to ArXiv as david.k@test.com / test1234. Visit the Starred page, remove the oldest starred entry, and report the title removed.
On ArXiv, register a new account (password 'test1234'). Star arXiv:2104.08821 from its abstract page and verify it shows up on /starred.
Log in to ArXiv as alice.j@test.com / test1234. Visit the Alerts page and create a new daily alert for category 'cs.AI'. Report the active alert list afterwards.
Log in as bob.c@test.com / test1234 on ArXiv. Subscribe to a weekly alert for category 'cs.CV' from the Alerts page and confirm the alert appears in the list.
Log in to ArXiv as carol.d@test.com / test1234. Cancel one existing alert from your Alerts page and report the alert's category code that you removed.
Log in to ArXiv as david.k@test.com / test1234. Create two alerts (cs.LG weekly and quant-ph daily) from the Alerts page and report both creation timestamps.
Register a new ArXiv account and create an alert for cs.IR with monthly frequency. Confirm the alert appears.
Log in to ArXiv as alice.j@test.com / test1234. Select two papers from your Library and create a BibTeX export. Report the export ID and the paper count.
On ArXiv, log in as bob.c@test.com / test1234. From the Export page, create a new export in BibTeX format using all papers in your 'Reading List' folder. Report the resulting export's status.
Log in as alice.j@test.com / test1234 on ArXiv. Visit the Export page, open the existing completed export, and download the BibTeX file. Report the first BibTeX entry's @article key.
Log in to ArXiv as carol.d@test.com / test1234. Create a citation export in RIS format from any 3 papers in your Library. Report the export_id assigned.
Log in to ArXiv as david.k@test.com / test1234. From the Export page, cancel one pending export and report the export_id that was cancelled.
On ArXiv, log in as alice.j@test.com / test1234. Reorder the papers in your most recent BibTeX export so that arXiv:2104.08821 comes first, then download and confirm the new order.
Open the ArXiv About page and list all members of arXiv's Leadership Team. Then visit the Help → How to Submit page and report the supported figure formats.

Open arXiv:2104.08821, navigate to its category page from the primary subject link, and report the latest announce_date on that category page.
On ArXiv, perform a search for 'language model', then refine the result by category cs.CL and sort by Most viewed. Report the top result's arXiv ID.
